message:
feat(tools,server,guard,db,share): cross-model review fixes, TUI expansion, and search v3 hardening

Multi-agent review artifacts (Gemini cross-model analysis):
- Add GEMINI_FIXES_2.md and GEMINI_FIXES_3.md documenting critical bug
  fixes found during cross-model review sessions
- Update GEMINI_FINAL_REPORT.md and GEMINI_STATUS.md with review progress

Core library (mcp-agent-mail-core):
- Add pattern_overlap module with CompiledPattern for glob-based file
  reservation overlap detection using globset, with normalization and
  symmetry tests

Tools (mcp-agent-mail-tools):
- Fix reply_message to enforce contact policy parity with send_message
  (previously replies bypassed contact checks entirely)
- Add recipient auto-registration support to reply_message
- Extract shared push_recipient logic for DRY recipient resolution
- Fix reservation_index and reservations modules (minor adjustments)

Server (mcp-agent-mail-server):
- Add init_search_bridge() for search v3 configuration initialization
- Expand TUI app with 461 lines of new functionality
- Expand TUI chrome with 168 lines of styling/rendering
- Fix static export and tool metrics minor issues

Guard (mcp-agent-mail-guard):
- Simplify and reduce guard logic by ~194 lines (refactoring)

Database tests:
- Expand filter_pagination tests with 297+ lines of new coverage
- Refactor diversity_dedup tests (formatting + style)
- Refactor load_concurrency tests
- Expand logging_redaction tests with 179+ lines

Search core:
- Add 367 lines of fault injection tests with MutableSource for CRUD
  transition integration testing

Share module:
- Refactor bundle, deploy, and scrub modules for cleaner error handling

E2E tests:
- Update search v3 shadow parity test script

Beads metadata and Cargo.lock updated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Expose user-visible degraded-mode diagnostics so users understand when results were produced under constrained budgets.

## Scope
- MCP response metadata for budget exhaustion, timeout stage, and fallback mode.
- TUI rendering for degraded status and concise remediation hints.
- Deterministic semantics so automation can branch on degradation signals.

## Deliverable
Transparent diagnostics for partial/degraded results without leaking restricted data.","acceptance_criteria":"1. MCP responses expose structured degraded-mode diagnostics including timeout stage, budget exhaustion reason, and fallback path.
2. TUI Search rendering (built on T6.2) shows degraded-state indicators and concise remediation hints consistently with MCP semantics.
3. Diagnostics remain policy-safe and never leak redacted or scope-restricted content.\n\nPlan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead","notes":"Implemented degraded-mode diagnostics surfacing across MCP tool responses and TUI Search UX.

 {"id":"br-2tnl.7","title":"[track] Search V3 Test Matrix, Relevance Eval, and Performance Gates","description":"## Purpose
Enforce quality rigor for Search V3 with deterministic tests, relevance evaluation, benchmark gates, and fault injection.

## Scope
- Unit and integration tests for parser/indexing/fusion/rerank correctness.
- MCP conformance expansion for mode/filter/scope contracts.
- Relevance benchmark corpus and metric pipeline.
- Performance benchmark gates with regression thresholds.
- Dedicated E2E scripts for stdio, HTTP, shadow parity, and resilience.
- Shared artifact logging harness for high-fidelity diagnostics.

## Output Contract
Every Search V3 test layer must emit actionable diagnostics, not just pass/fail booleans.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit, integration, conformance, relevance, performance, and fault-injection suites are in place.
- E2E scripts exist for stdio/http/shadow-parity/resilience with explicit assertion budgets and deterministic logs.
- Benchmarks emit actionable pass/fail signals tied to SLO thresholds.
- Regression detection for quality and latency is automated.
- CI publishes structured artifacts for failing Search V3 test runs.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"track","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:36:26.941880068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:55.715884226Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","relevance","search","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.7","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:36:26.941880068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.7","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:35.640892479Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.7","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:37.433147313Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.7","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:37.635239354Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.7","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:37.835452542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":235,"issue_id":"br-2tnl.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Track 7 reasoning:

- Search rewrites fail quietly without strong relevance and perf harnesses; this track prevents silent regressions.
- We need both algorithmic correctness tests and corpus-level relevance metrics.
- Benchmarks must include resource budgets and regression thresholds, not just one-off timing snapshots.
- Fault injection is included because index/model/daemon failures are expected in real operation and must be graceful.","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:39:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-2tnl.7.1","title":"T7.1: Build unit test suite for parser, filters, fusion, and rerank math","description":"## Task
Add unit tests for lexical parser/filter compiler/fusion math/rerank score blending.

## Scope
- parser edge cases.
- filter composition correctness.
- deterministic ordering and tie-break behavior.
- explain payload invariants.

## Deliverable
Comprehensive unit safety net for search core algorithms.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
1. Unit tests cover lexical parser normalization, metadata filter compilation, hybrid fusion math, rerank blend calculations, and deadline-budget decision logic from T5.9.
2. Fixtures include deterministic expected scores/ranks and budget fallback outcomes with readable diffs on failures.
3. CI runs these unit tests on every Search V3 change path with no flaky behavior.\n\n

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Detailed diagnostics artifacts are produced (scenario IDs, timing breakdowns, failure reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","notes":"Logging requirement: emit detailed assertion traces and machine-readable JSON summaries under test artifacts so failures are reproducible and diagnosable without rerunning under debugger.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:06.096462880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T09:51:07.692439444Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T09:51:07.692374773Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["search","tests","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.7.1","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:19.755768430Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.7.1","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:20.115664427Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.7.1","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:08:14.581308180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.7.1","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.5.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:30:18.872415758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2tnl.7.1","depends_on_id":"br-2tnl.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:38:06.096462880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Create `tests/e2e/test_search_v3_resilience.sh` covering operational failure paths.

## Scope
- Simulate missing/corrupt lexical and vector index artifacts.
- Simulate unavailable embedder/reranker/daemon components.
- Validate fallback behavior, user-visible errors, and recovery actions.
- Require high-fidelity logs and post-failure diagnostics via T7.7 harness.

## Test Depth
Target >= 70 assertions including failure injection and recovery validation.

## Deliverable
Resilience E2E script proving graceful degradation and recoverability.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Resilience E2E script validates behavior when index, model, or daemon components degrade during live query traffic.
2. Assertions verify graceful degradation, clear error surfaces, and successful restoration paths.

## What to build
Seed a benchmark database with realistic data (project, agents, messages) using
direct DB calls instead of spawning 62 CLI subprocesses.

## Current bash behavior (bench_cli.sh lines 144-177)
```bash
# 62 subprocess spawns:
am agents register --project /tmp/bench --name BlueLake ...
am agents register --project /tmp/bench --name RedFox ...
for i in $(seq 1 49); do
    am mail send --project /tmp/bench --from BlueLake --to RedFox ...
done
for i in $(seq 1 10); do
    am mail send --project /tmp/bench --from RedFox --to BlueLake ...
done
```
This takes 3-5 seconds. Direct DB calls should take <50ms.

## Rust implementation
```rust
fn seed_bench_db(db_path: &Path) -> Result<(), Error> {
    let conn = open_db_sync(db_path)?;
    ensure_schema(&conn)?;

    // Create project
    let project_id = insert_project(&conn, "/tmp/bench")?;

    // Register agents
    let blue_lake_id = insert_agent(&conn, project_id, "BlueLake", "bench", "bench")?;
    let red_fox_id = insert_agent(&conn, project_id, "RedFox", "bench", "bench")?;

    // Send 50 messages BlueLake → RedFox
    for i in 0..50 {
        insert_message(&conn, project_id, blue_lake_id, red_fox_id,
            &format!("bench message {i}"), &format!("body of message {i}"))?;
    }

    // Send 10 replies RedFox → BlueLake
    for i in 0..10 {
        insert_message(&conn, project_id, red_fox_id, blue_lake_id,
            &format!("reply {i}"), &format!("reply body {i}"))?;
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

## Implementation notes
- Use the existing open_db_sync() pattern from the CLI crate
- Import schema creation from mcp-agent-mail-db
- Use transactions for bulk inserts (single commit = fastest)
- The seeded data should be identical to what bench_cli.sh produces
  (same project path, agent names, message subjects/bodies)
- Create a temp directory for the bench DB (cleaned up after benchmarks)

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/bench.rs (seed_bench_db function)

## Dependencies
- Needs the DB layer's insert functions (queries.rs or raw SQL)
- Uses sync SQLite (sqlmodel_sqlite), not async pool

Use the existing open_db_sync() pattern from the CLI crate.

 {"id":"br-3k16e","title":"R1.4: Implement TOON serializers for Agent Mail domain types (messages, agents, reservations as tabular arrays)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:16:48.439159076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T04:47:21.113670953Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T04:47:21.113648330Z","close_reason":"15 TOON-friendly domain types in robot.rs with Serialize derives + toon round-trip tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3k16e","depends_on_id":"br-3i7fz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:17:47.343307729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":299,"issue_id":"br-3k16e","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R1.4: TOON Serializers for Domain Types

## What
Implement `Serialize` (serde) for all domain types that robot commands return, ensuring TOON-friendly structure. The key insight: TOON's tabular array format (`items[N]{col1,col2}: ...`) works best when arrays contain uniform structs with flat fields.

## Where
- Core model types in `mcp-agent-mail-core/src/models.rs` (if not already Serialize)
- New response types in `mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/` for robot-specific views

## Domain Types Needed
Each robot command returns a specific data struct. These must be flat and tabular-friendly:

1. **StatusData** — inbox_summary, recent_activity, anomalies[], my_reservations[], top_threads[]
2. **InboxEntry** — id, priority, from, subject, thread, age, ack_status, importance
3. **TimelineEvent** — seq, timestamp, kind, summary, source
4. **OverviewProject** — slug, unread, urgent, ack_overdue, reservations
5. **ThreadMessage** — position, from, to, age, importance, ack, subject, body
6. **SearchResult** — id, relevance, from, subject, thread, snippet, age
7. **MessageContext** — full message + thread position + adjacent summaries
8. **ReservationEntry** — agent, path, exclusive, remaining, granted_at
9. **MetricEntry** — name, calls, errors, error_pct, avg_ms, p95_ms, p99_ms
10. **HealthProbe** — name, status, latency_ms, detail
11. **AnomalyCard** — severity, confidence, category, headline, rationale, remediation
12. **AgentRow** — name, program, model, last_active, msg_count, status
13. **ContactRow** — from, to, status, policy, reason, updated
14. **ProjectRow** — slug, path, agents, messages, reservations, created
15. **AttachmentRow** — type, size, sender, subject, message_id, project

## TOON Optimization Guidelines
- Keep structs FLAT (no nested objects in tabular arrays)
- Use short field names where possible (agent not agent_name)
- Timestamps as relative strings ("5m ago") for TOON, ISO-8601 for JSON
- Boolean fields as true/false (TOON renders compactly)
- Numeric fields without units (units in column header)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 15 types derive Serialize
- Unit test: serialize each type to JSON, convert to TOON, verify parseable
- TOON output for a 5-row array uses <50% of the JSON token count

+# Gemini Fixes Summary (Part 2) - 2026-02-16
+
+## Critical Bug Fixes
+
+### 1. Git Author Batching Bug in `CommitCoalescer`
+- **Component:** `crates/mcp-agent-mail-storage`
+- **Issue:** The `CommitCoalescer` blindly batched up to `COALESCER_MAX_BATCH_SIZE` commit requests per repository without checking if they originated from the same git author. `coalescer_commit_batch` then used the author info from the *first* request for the entire batch commit. This meant that if multiple agents (or users) triggered commits to the same repo in rapid succession, the resulting git commit would misattribute authorship for all but the first requester.
+- **Fix:** Modified `coalescer_pool_worker` in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-storage/src/lib.rs` to inspect the author of the next candidate item in the queue. If the author (name or email) differs from the current batch, the batch is closed immediately. This ensures every git commit has a single, correct author, preserving audit trail integrity even under high concurrency.
+
+## Verification
+- Reviewed `coalescer_commit_batch` to confirm it uses the first request's author, validating that the worker-side split is the correct fix location.
+- Verified that the fallback path (sequential processing) correctly respects individual authors.

+# Gemini Fixes Summary (Part 3) - 2026-02-16
+
+## Critical Bug Fixes
+
+### 1. `reply_message` Logic Parity with `send_message`
+- **Component:** `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools`
+- **Issue:** The `reply_message` implementation lacked two critical features present in `send_message`:
+    1.  **Contact Policy Enforcement:** Replies bypassed the contact policy checks, allowing agents to message blocked or non-consented recipients simply by replying.
+    2.  **Recipient Auto-Registration:** Replies to new recipients (e.g. adding a CC) failed with `RECIPIENT_NOT_FOUND` instead of auto-registering them if configured.
+- **Fix:** Refactored `reply_message` in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/messaging.rs` to use the shared `push_recipient` logic (enabling auto-registration) and copied the rigorous contact enforcement block from `send_message` (checking thread history, file reservations, and contact links).
+
+## Verification
+- Verified `reply_message` now performs the same resolution and enforcement steps as `send_message`.
+- Confirmed `auto_contact_if_blocked` behavior logic is included (using config default).
